description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-slung profile with a prominent cross-gabled roof in a dark brown color. The exterior walls are clad in a light stone or brick material, giving the structure a sturdy and classic appearance. Large overhanging eaves and a wraparound porch supported by stone columns are visible, providing sheltered outdoor space. Multiple windows of varying sizes are arranged asymmetrically, with some grouped together and others spaced apart, enhancing the residential character. The building appears to have two stories in one section, with the main living area likely on the ground floor and additional rooms above. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and modern bungalow elements, suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A stone-clad bungalow with a dark cross-gabled roof, wraparound porch, and asymmetrical window arrangement. The structure combines traditional and modern elements with two stories in one section.
description_view_0: This view highlights the side and rear of the bungalow, showing the cross-gabled roof and the stone exterior with several windows.
description_view_1: The front facade is visible here, featuring the main entrance, wraparound porch, and a prominent roofline with dormer windows.
description_view_2: This angle shows the opposite side, emphasizing the porch area and the extension of the roof over the outdoor space.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the back wall with fewer windows and the continuation of the stone cladding and roof structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped and spaced windows
